LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, BASED ON VARIANCE BETWEEN PLAINTIFF'S GOVERNMENT CLAIM AND COMPLAINT. SEE FALL RIVER JOINT UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. SUPERIOR COURT, 206, CAL.APP.3D 431. THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THE DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR #10276
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF RICHARDSON, JEYNITHA TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

Notice Of Motion And Otion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Mar-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion for Judgment on The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice. Appearances: Greer Smith, Esq. of the Law Offices of Greer Smith, (415) 409-7123, appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; Joshua S. White, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4259, appearing for Defendant, CCSF, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted without leave to amend, based on variance between plaintiff's government claim and complaint. See Fall River Joint Unified School District vs. Superior Court, 206, Cal.App.3d 431. There is no reasonable possibility that the defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR 10276
